message:
style(readiness): rustfmt reformat readiness vocabulary

Pure rustfmt adjustment on the recently-landed readiness vocabulary
module (bead ibuuh.9 / src/search/readiness.rs).

Changes are entirely width/layout:
- Two-arg constructors `ReadinessSnapshot::new` and
  `with_last_search_refinement` collapse from multi-line to
  single-line argument lists (both fit in 100 cols).
- Match arms in `recommended_action` for
  `FastTierReady | HybridReady` collapse from multi-line back into
  the canonical "one arm per line, braces on same line" form.
- Test tables splitting long tuple literals across multiple lines
  (the `(LexicalReadinessState::CorruptQuarantined,
  "corrupt_quarantined")` type cases) now match rustfmt's preferred
  align-rightward multi-line shape.
- `assert_eq!(snap.recommended_action(),
  RecommendedAction::RepairLexicalNow)` split across lines because
  the full call exceeds 100 cols.

No semantic or behavior change; pure formatting.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
